name: PULMONARY ATRESIA
definition: CONGENITAL ABSENCE OF PULMONARY ARTERY atretic pulmonary valve with underdeveloped pulmonary artery distally AGENESIS OF PULMONARY ARTERY atresia of mediastinal portion of right / left pulmonary artery (usually opposite the side of part of spectrum of tetralogy of Fallot (TOF)
dominant_finding: short pulmonary a. terminating within 1 cm of its origin
distribution: complete absence of mediastinal portion of affected pulmonary artery
discriminating_features: complete absence of mediastinal portion of affected pulmonary artery
short pulmonary a. terminating within 1 cm of its origin
hypoplastic ipsilateral lung + hyperinflated contralateral lung rotating across midline
abundant systemic collaterals to affected lung ← dilated bronchial arteries, subclavian
linear peripheral lung opacities = transpleural vascular collaterals

CXR: reticular network of vessels on affected side ← systemic collateral circulation from
rib notching from prominence of intercostal arteries ← large transpleural collateral vessels
flow reversal in ductus arteriosus + main pulmonary artery (most reliable)
CT: complete absence of mediastinal portion of affected pulmonary artery
short pulmonary a. terminating within 1 cm of its origin
hypoplastic ipsilateral lung + hyperinflated contralateral lung rotating across midline
abundant systemic collaterals to affected lung ← dilated bronchial arteries, subclavian
linear peripheral lung opacities = transpleural vascular collaterals
moderately ↑ RA (depending on size of ASD)
cardiomegaly ← increase in size of LV + RA

clinical.Rx: prolonged infusion of prostaglandin E, stent placement into ductus, modified Blalock-
